filter_pushdown.note: 10471 = epoch days for 1998-09-02; ~15M of 60M rows qualify

aggregation_detail.indexing: result[returnflag_code][linestatus_code], where returnflag_code in {0,1,2} from int16_t dict encoding, linestatus_code in {0,1}
aggregation_detail.thread_local: Each of 64 threads owns a private flat_array[3][2] — no synchronization during scan
aggregation_detail.merge: Sequential merge of 64 thread-local arrays into global result: 64 * 6 = 384 additions (negligible cost)

data_structures.output_buffer: flat_array[6] (6 output rows, sorted by returnflag ASC, linestatus ASC)

parallelism.synchronization_detail: Each thread accumulates into its own flat_array[3][2]; after all morsels complete, main thread merges all 64 local arrays sequentially — zero lock contention during scan phase

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600] then [int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t block_size] x 600
index_usage.expected_skip_rate: 20-30% of 600 blocks skipped (late 1998 blocks where all shipdates > 1998-09-02)
index_usage.usage: Load full zone map (600 entries, ~7KB) at startup. For each block, if zone_entry.min > 10471 skip entire block (100K rows). Otherwise scan block with row-level filter.

scan_detail.pass_description: One sequential pass over all non-skipped blocks, reading all 7 required columns (l_shipdate, l_returnflag, l_linestatus, l_quantity, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_tax) per block in column-major order
scan_detail.filter_application: Apply l_shipdate <= 10471 row-level within each qualifying block using SIMD (AVX2: compare 8x int32_t per instruction)
column_load_order: l_shipdate (int32_t) — filter column, loaded and tested first per row to gate remaining column reads
l_returnflag (int16_t) — GROUP BY key, used as array index dim 0
l_linestatus (int16_t) — GROUP BY key, used as array index dim 1
scan_detail.late_materialization: Not applied — all 7 columns needed for every qualifying row; single-pass fused avoids column re-read overhead vs multi-pass

simd_filter.operation: Compare 8x int32_t l_shipdate values against broadcast(10471) per vector iteration
simd_filter.result: 8-bit mask of qualifying rows within vector window; gather remaining columns for set bits

scalar_expression_plan.sum_disc_price_raw: l_extendedprice * (100 - l_discount) / 100  [integer arithmetic, divide by 100 to re-scale]
scalar_expression_plan.sum_charge_raw: l_extendedprice * (100 - l_discount) / 100 * (100 + l_tax) / 100  [two-step integer, avoids overflow on int64_t for SF10]

output_sort.detail: Sort 6 rows by (returnflag_code ASC, linestatus_code ASC). Since codes are already small integers, this is a constant-time operation. Dictionary decode returnflag and linestatus codes to strings at output time only.

physical_plan.memory_pattern_detail: Each thread reads morsels in sequential column-file order. L3 cache (44MB) easily holds thread-local aggregation state (6 x 6 x 8 bytes per thread = trivial). Column data streams through LLC as a read-once sequential pass.

phase_breakdown.column_scan_io: ~60-70ms (7 columns x 60M rows x ~8 bytes = ~3.3GB, HDD sequential, zone-map reduces by ~25% to ~2.5GB effective I/O)
phase_breakdown.filter_and_aggregate: ~10-15ms (SIMD filter + 6-group flat-array accumulation, fully parallelized across 64 cores)

estimated_cost.bottleneck: I/O bound on HDD — sequential column reads dominate; zone map pruning and 64-core parallelism minimize compute overhead to near-zero relative to disk bandwidth
